title: Wireshark
html:
Wireshark

Open-source packet-capture / protocol-analysis tool. The canonical instrument of Yahir's June 2025 network forensics burst at OSU — every screenshot in that 7-capture cluster is a Wireshark window, run on a Windows VM in a malware analysis lab context.

Where it shows up

All seven appearances are inside the same 60-hour all-nighter cluster (June 9-11, 2025):

  • 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 12 54 20 pm 69ac5054 — initial setup
  • 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 09 50 pm fa1a25f4 — filter syntax
  • 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 09 58 pm 90b6652f — follow-tcp-stream
  • 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 1 13 19 pm d91bef48 — HTTP traffic
  • 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 12 15 12 am ba57b115 — past-midnight session
  • 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 05 42 am f1abc915 — canonical VM lab capture
  • 2025 06 11 screenshot 2025 06 11 at 7 45 30 pm f43b20a6 — wrap-up

Why it matters

Wireshark is the practical learning tool that anchors the abstract network forensics concept — the place Yahir converts security-coursework theory into a concrete TCP-stream-following motor skill. Sits next to DVWA as the two hands-on tools of the summer 2025 OSU security capstone era. Both are Yahir-on-an-M1-Mac doing the lab the way a professional incident-responder would do it.

Connections

network forensics · malware analysis lab · dvwa · mobile malware · surveillance state · reverse engineering · cs coursework · osu · m1 mac
